steps.title: Apply the product property of cube roots
Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $y^2$. This omits the $x$ factor entirely. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $x \times y^2$. Multiplying the two simplified factors gives $x \cdot y^2$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • Choice C: $y^3$. $\sqrt[3]{y^6} = y^2$, not $y^3$, and the $x$ is missing. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: $x \times y^3$. The exponent on $y$ is $\frac{6}{3} = 2$, not 3. Eliminated.
